description: Family clustering tests: verifies that structurally related materials cluster together and unrelated ones separate

tests.description: Cubic perovskites (SrTiO3, BaTiO3) should form one family; ilmenite (TiFeO3) a separate family; LaNiO3 R-3c perovskite should be distinct from ilmenite

assertions.description: SrTiO3 and BaTiO3 are both cubic Pm-3m perovskites and should cluster together
Perovskite and ilmenite are structurally unrelated
Rhombohedral perovskite and ilmenite are structurally unrelated

tests.description: Three distinct perovskite symmetry classes: cubic (SrTiO3, BaZrO3), GdFeO3-type Pbnm (LaFeO3, GdFeO3), and unrelated ilmenite (TiFeO3) as out-group

assertions.description: SrTiO3 and BaZrO3 are both cubic Pm-3m perovskites
LaFeO3 and GdFeO3 are both Pbnm GdFeO3-type distorted perovskites
Ilmenite TiFeO3 should not cluster with any perovskite
Ilmenite TiFeO3 should not cluster with GdFeO3-type perovskites either

tests.description: Corundum (Al2O3, Cr2O3) and ilmenite (TiFeO3, MgTiO3) should each form a family — or a joint corundum-type family since they are topologically equivalent (element-agnostic). KSbO3 and AgSbO3 ilmenite pair as control.

assertions.description: Al2O3 and Cr2O3 share R-3c corundum topology and should cluster together
TiFeO3 and MgTiO3 are both R-3 ilmenite and should cluster together
KSbO3 and AgSbO3 (ilmenite-structured, mp-16293 and mp-545999) should cluster together

tests.description: TiO2, SnO2, RuO2 are all rutile-type (P42/mnm) and should form one family; SrTiO3 (perovskite) and TiFeO3 (ilmenite) as out-groups

assertions.description: TiO2, SnO2, and RuO2 are all rutile-type — should cluster together
SrTiO3 (perovskite, CN=12/6 with corner-sharing and CN=6 O) should not join the rutile family (CN=6/3, edge+corner-sharing)
TiFeO3 ilmenite should not join the rutile family

tests.description: NaCl, MgO, NiO are all rocksalt (Fm-3m, all CN=6 corner-sharing); TiO2 (rutile, CN=3 O) and TiFeO3 (ilmenite, face-sharing) as out-groups

assertions.description: NaCl, MgO, and NiO are all rocksalt — identical corner-only octahedral topology
TiO2 rutile (CN=3 O, edge-sharing chains) should not join the rocksalt family
TiFeO3 ilmenite (face+edge-sharing) should not join the rocksalt family
